summary: The patient has a history of non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC), which significantly increases risk for future bone metastases. Bone metastases commonly present with osteolysis leading to lytic lesions seen on imaging — these findings align well with both typical pathology and reported incidence rates post-treatment. Therefore, even without clear warning signs, malignancy must remain part of differential consideration until ruled out via appropriate testing.
